executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has escalated to an unprecedented level with Iran striking European territory for the first time (Cyprus/British base) and killing at least 170 students/teachers in Iranian schools. The death of Supreme Leader Khamenei has triggered full regional mobilization with Hezbollah formally entering the war and targeting Haifa. US officials express skepticism about regime change feasibility while Trump signals 4-5 weeks of continued hostilities. The conflict has expanded beyond the Gulf to include Cyprus, Iraq, and direct Iranian strikes on Israeli civilian infrastructure.
whats_unique: First Iranian ballistic missile strike on European territory: two missiles fired at Cyprus and drone strike on British Akrotiri base — confirmed by multiple sources
At least 170 students and teachers killed in US-Israeli airstrikes on Iranian educational facilities (major civilian escalation)
IRGC claims shot down American MQ-9 Reaper drone over Isfahan province using Iranian air defense system
Hezbollah formally announces entry into war, targeting Israeli military installations at Haifa in retaliation for Khamenei's killing
UNESCO condemns Israeli airstrike on girls' school in Minab that killed approximately 180 children — single-source but highly significant if confirmed
confirmed_events: UK authorizes US to use British bases for limited strikes on Iranian missile storage depots and launchers — confirmed by multiple sources
IRGC launched ballistic missile volley at USS Abraham Lincoln carrier — first known Iranian attack on a US aircraft carrier — confirmed by multiple sources
Iranian missile/drone strikes hit UAE including AWS data centre causing fire and power outage — confirmed by multiple sources
Trump indicates US-Iran confrontation may last four to five weeks, marking extended timeline expectation
Hezbollah launched rockets and drones at Israel opening new front following US-Israeli attack on Iran
unverified_emerging: Unverified reports of violent explosions in Dubai and Doha — source: Hezbollah Electronic Army Telegram channel — attribution and details pending
US military briefly acknowledged Iranian strike on Haifa oil refinery before deleting post — requires independent corroboration
Violent explosions reported in occupied Palestine creating large crater — source: multiple Telegram channels
Israeli strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs causing destruction — escalating Lebanon front

people_travel: Cathay Pacific has suspended all Middle East operations following the US-Israeli attack on Iran — confirmed commercial aviation disruption
Air raid sirens activated across Israel including Jerusalem and Ashdod indicating ongoing projectile threats
supply_chain: Norwegian insurers Skuld and Gard refuse war risk insurance for vessels following attack on Iran — major shipping/maritime disruption
Strait of Hormuz attacks causing oil price surge to $80 and Asian stocks drop — confirmed market impact
market_macro: Oil prices surge to $80/barrel amid Strait of Hormuz attacks on several ships — confirmed
Asian stocks fall on Iran conflict escalation — confirmed

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is on an extended trajectory with Trump signaling 4-5 weeks of hostilities. With Khamenei killed and Iran refusing negotiations, the military escalation will likely continue with expanded US-UK strikes on Iranian missile capabilities and Iranian retaliation targeting Gulf states, Israel, and now European territory (Cyprus). Hezbollah's formal entry into the war adds a significant new front. The killing of 170+ students/teachers in educational facilities may trigger additional international condemnation but is unlikely to halt military operations.
escalation_risks: Iranian proxy forces (Iraqi Shia militias, Houthis) intensifying attacks on US bases across Iraq and Gulf region
Direct Iranian strikes on Gulf state energy infrastructure (Saudi Arabia, Qatar) potentially triggering wider regional involvement
US/Israeli strikes expanding to target Iranian nuclear facilities with potential WMD dimension
Hezbollah opening full northern front against Israel risking Lebanon ceasefire collapse
Russian or Chinese intervention/military support for Iran increasing global confrontation risk
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: With Iran formally refusing negotiations through Supreme National Security Council Secretary Larijani and US officials skeptical of regime change despite military successes, de-escalation pathways appear extremely limited in the near term. Any ceasefire would require both parties to claim sufficient victory while saving face — unlikely until one side suffers decisive military defeat or economic collapse. International mediation (UN, Russia, China) remains possible but neither side has signaled willingness to accept third-party intervention.
